walkthrough.title: Choose the Right Map
walkthrough.text: Let's get Small World set up. First, find the two double-sided game boards. Each side shows a different map designed for a specific player count. Look at the symbol next to the Game Turn track on each map to match it to your group size. Two players, three players, four players, or five players. Pick the correct side and place it in the center of the table where everyone can reach it.

walkthrough.title: Place the Game Turn Marker
walkthrough.text: Find the Game Turn marker. Place it on the first spot of the Game Turn track printed on the board. This track monitors the game's progress. Depending on your player count, the game will last 8, 9, or 10 turns. The game ends at the end of the turn in which the marker reaches the last position on the track.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Storage Tray
walkthrough.text: Pull the removable storage tray with all its Race tokens out of the box and place it open next to the board map, within easy reach of all players. The tray has a compartment for each of the 14 races. You will be drawing Race tokens from here throughout the game. Keep it accessible.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle and Lay Out Race Banners
walkthrough.text: Find all 14 Race banners. These are the rectangular pieces that are colored on one side and grayed-out on the other. Shuffle them and draw five at random. Lay these five face up, colored side visible, in a single column next to the board. Place the remaining Race banners face up in a single stack at the bottom of the column. You should now see five individual banners in a column with a stack below them, making six combos visible total.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle and Lay Out Special Power Badges
walkthrough.text: Now find the 20 Special Power badges. These are the round-edged pieces that pair with the Race banners. Shuffle them and place one to the left of each Race banner in the column, with the round edge of the badge fitting into the banner's round opening. Stack the remaining badges in a pile to the left of the Race banners stack at the bottom. You should now have six Race banner and Special Power badge combos visible face up on the table.

walkthrough.title: Place Lost Tribe Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the 18 Lost Tribe tokens. These represent remnants of long-forgotten civilizations. Place one Lost Tribe token on each Region of the map that features a Lost Tribe symbol. These tokens will act as defenders that players must overcome during their initial conquests.

walkthrough.title: Place Mountain Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the 9 Mountain tokens. Place one Mountain token on each Region of the map that features a Mountain symbol. Mountains are immovable and remain in place throughout the game. They provide an extra layer of defense for whoever controls the Region, requiring one additional Race token to conquer.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Starting Coins
walkthrough.text: Give each player five "1" Victory coins to start with. Place all remaining Victory coins, including all the "3"s, "5"s, and "10"s, in a Victory stash next to the board within easy reach of all players. The game contains 109 Victory Coins total: 30 tens, 24 fives, 20 threes, and 35 ones. Players keep their coins hidden from other players at all times. If you need change during the game, swap with the stash anytime.

walkthrough.title: Set Out Game Pieces
walkthrough.text: Place the remaining game pieces near the board where players can reach them: 10 Troll Lairs, 6 Fortresses, 5 Encampments, 2 Holes-in-the-Ground, 2 Heroes, and 1 Dragon. Also set out the single custom Reinforcement Die. These pieces will be used by various Race and Special Power abilities during the game.

walkthrough.title: Hand Out Summary Sheets and Determine First Player
walkthrough.text: Give each player one Player Summary sheet for reference. Place one extra summary sheet in the center as a game turn reference for the group. Now determine the first player. According to the rulebook, the player whose ears are the most pointed starts the game. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player. You are ready to begin.

summary.title: Choose the Right Map
bullets: Two double-sided boards, one map per player count (2, 3, 4, or 5)
Match the symbol next to the Game Turn track to your player count
Place the correct map in the center of the table

summary.title: Place the Game Turn Marker
bullets: Place the Game Turn marker on the first spot of the track
Game lasts 8, 9, or 10 turns depending on the map
Game ends when the marker reaches the final spot and all players finish that turn

summary.title: Set Out the Storage Tray
bullets: Pull the removable Race token storage tray from the box
Place it open next to the board within easy reach of all players
14 races, each with its own compartment: 168 Race tokens total

Draw 5 and lay them face up (colored side) in a single column
Stack remaining banners face up at the bottom of the column

summary.title: Shuffle and Lay Out Special Power Badges
bullets: Shuffle all 20 Special Power badges
Place one badge to the left of each visible Race banner
Stack remaining badges to the left of the Race banner stack
6 total Race + Special Power combos should be visible

summary.title: Place Lost Tribe Tokens
bullets: 18 Lost Tribe tokens total
Place one on each Region with a Lost Tribe symbol
Lost Tribes are defenders that must be conquered

summary.title: Place Mountain Tokens
bullets: 9 Mountain tokens total
Place one on each Region with a Mountain symbol
Mountains are immovable and add +1 defense permanently

summary.title: Distribute Starting Coins
bullets: Each player receives five "1" Victory coins
Remaining coins form the Victory stash: 30 tens, 24 fives, 20 threes, 35 ones (109 total)
Coins are always kept hidden from other players

summary.title: Set Out Game Pieces
bullets: Place near the board: 10 Troll Lairs, 6 Fortresses, 5 Encampments
Also: 2 Holes-in-the-Ground, 2 Heroes, 1 Dragon
Set out the 1 custom Reinforcement Die

summary.title: Hand Out Summary Sheets and Determine First Player
bullets: Give each player 1 Player Summary sheet (6 total, 1 extra for group reference)
First player: whoever has the most pointed ears (or choose randomly)
Play proceeds clockwise

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Small World is played over a set number of turns depending on your player count, tracked by the Game Turn marker. On each turn, every player picks a fantasy Race and Special Power combination, uses their Race tokens to conquer Regions on the map, and scores Victory coins for the Regions they occupy. The twist is that your race will eventually become overextended, and you will need to put it In Decline and pick a new one. The player with the most Victory coins at the end wins.

walkthrough.title: First Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: During the very first turn, each player does three things in order. First, they pick a Race and Special Power combo from the six visible on the table. Second, they conquer some Regions on the map with their Race tokens. Third, they score Victory coins for each Region they occupy. Let's walk through each of these in detail.

walkthrough.title: Picking a Race and Special Power Combo
walkthrough.text: You select one of the six visible Race and Special Power combos. The first combo at the top of the column is free. Each combo further down the column costs one additional Victory coin. You pay by dropping one of your Victory coins on each combo above the one you want. For example, the third combo down costs 2 coins, one placed on each of the two combos above it. If a combo you select has coins on it from previous players who passed it up, you pocket those coins. After selecting, slide the remaining combos up to fill the gap and reveal a new combo from the stacks.

walkthrough.title: Collecting Your Race Tokens
walkthrough.text: Once you pick your combo, take matching Race tokens from the storage tray. The number you receive equals the value printed on your Race banner plus the value printed on your Special Power badge. For example, if your Race banner shows 6 and your Special Power badge shows 2, you take 8 Race tokens. Unless stated otherwise by your Race or Special Power, these are the only tokens you will have for this race during the entire game.

walkthrough.title: First Conquest: Entering the Map
walkthrough.text: When your race enters the map for the first time, you must conquer one of the border Regions. A border Region is one adjacent to the edge of the board, or one whose shore is on a Sea adjacent to the board edge. This is the only time the border rule applies. On following turns, you expand from Regions you already occupy.

walkthrough.title: Conquering a Region
walkthrough.text: To conquer a Region, you must deploy Race tokens equal to 2 plus 1 for each Encampment, Fortress, Mountain, or Troll's Lair marker in the Region, plus 1 for each Lost Tribe token or other player's Race token already there. For example, an empty Region costs 2 tokens. A Region with 1 Lost Tribe costs 3 tokens. A Region with 1 Lost Tribe and 1 Mountain costs 4 tokens. Seas and Lakes can never normally be conquered. You must always have at least 1 Race token available to initiate any conquest.

walkthrough.title: Enemy Losses and Withdrawals
walkthrough.text: When you conquer a Region occupied by another player's Active Race tokens, that player takes all their tokens back in hand, permanently discards one token back to the storage tray, and keeps the rest to redeploy in any Region still occupied by their race at the end of your turn. The redeployment Regions do not need to be adjacent. If a Region has only a single defending token, such as a Lost Tribe or an In Decline token, that single token is simply discarded.

walkthrough.title: Following Conquests
walkthrough.text: After your first conquest, you may continue conquering additional Regions, provided you have enough Race tokens left. Each new Region must be adjacent to, meaning sharing a border with, a Region you already occupy with your active Race tokens, unless your Race or Special Power says otherwise. You place your Race tokens in each conquered Region immediately and they stay there until troop redeployment at the end of your turn.

walkthrough.title: Final Conquest and the Reinforcement Die
walkthrough.text: For your final conquest attempt of the turn, if you do not have enough tokens to conquer a Region outright but are only 1, 2, or 3 tokens short, you may roll the Reinforcement Die. First select the target Region, then roll the die. The die has faces showing 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, and 3. If the die result plus your remaining tokens equals or exceeds the conquest cost, you succeed and place your tokens there. If not, deploy your remaining tokens in a Region you already occupy. Either way, your conquests end immediately after this roll.

walkthrough.title: Troop Redeployment
walkthrough.text: Once your conquests for the turn are done, you may freely redeploy your Race tokens on the board. Move them from one Region to any other Region you occupy, not necessarily adjacent or contiguous. The only rule is that at least one Race token must remain in each Region you wish to keep under your control. This is your chance to fortify key positions and leave vulnerable Regions lightly guarded.

walkthrough.title: Scoring Victory Coins
walkthrough.text: After redeployment, you score. You receive 1 Victory coin from the stash for each Region your Race tokens occupy on the map. You may also collect additional bonus coins from your Race power and Special Power. For example, Humans earn 1 bonus coin per Farmland Region, and the Merchant Special Power earns 1 bonus coin per Region occupied. Coins from In Decline tokens also count: 1 coin per Region, but typically no bonus from Race or Special Power unless explicitly stated.

walkthrough.title: Following Turns: Ready Your Troops
walkthrough.text: On all turns after the first, at the start of your turn you Ready your Troops. Leave one Race token in each Region you occupy and take all other Active Race tokens back into your hand. These are the tokens you will use to conquer new Regions this turn. You may also choose to abandon Regions entirely, leaving no tokens there, to free up more troops. Abandoned Regions no longer belong to you.

walkthrough.title: Following Turns: Expand or Decline
walkthrough.text: On each subsequent turn, you must choose one of two paths. You can expand through new conquests, following the same conquest rules as before but without the border restriction. Or you can put your race In Decline and prepare to select a new race on your next turn. You cannot do both in the same turn, with the exception of the Stout Special Power which lets you decline after conquesting.

walkthrough.title: Entering In Decline
walkthrough.text: To put your race In Decline, flip your Race banner to its grayed-out side. Discard the Special Power badge that was associated with it, as it no longer has any effect, unless it says otherwise like the Spirit power. In each Region your race occupies, flip a single Race token to its In Decline side and remove all other tokens of that race from the board, returning them to the storage tray. You can only have one race In Decline at a time. If you had a previous In Decline race, remove those tokens first.

walkthrough.title: Scoring When Declining
walkthrough.text: The turn you go In Decline, you make no conquests. Your turn ends immediately after entering In Decline and scoring. You still score 1 Victory coin for each Region your newly In Decline tokens occupy, but you score no bonus coins from your now-inactive Race banner or discarded Special Power. On your next turn, you will pick a brand new Race and Special Power combo and start fresh, following the same rules as the first turn. You will now collect coins from both your new Active race and your In Decline tokens.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: When the Game Turn marker reaches the last spot on the track, all players play one final turn. Then everyone reveals their hidden Victory coins and tallies their total. The player with the most coins wins. In case of a tie, the player with the most Race tokens, both Active and In Decline, still on the board is the winner.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Game lasts 8-10 turns depending on player count
Pick a Race + Special Power combo, conquer Regions, score coins
Eventually put your race In Decline and pick a new one
Most Victory coins at game end wins

Pay by dropping 1 coin on each combo above your selection
Pocket any coins already sitting on your chosen combo
Slide remaining combos up, reveal new combo from stacks

Example: banner 6 + badge 2 = 8 tokens
These are your only tokens for this race unless powers say otherwise

Empty Region = 2 tokens; Region with 1 Lost Tribe = 3 tokens
Seas and Lakes cannot normally be conquered
Must always have at least 1 Race token to initiate a conquest

bullets: Defending player takes tokens back, permanently discards 1 to the tray
Remaining tokens redeploy to any Region their race still occupies

bullets: Continue conquering as long as you have Race tokens

Select target Region BEFORE rolling

bullets: Freely move tokens between any Regions you occupy

Cannot do both (exception: Stout Special Power)

bullets: Flip Race banner to grayed-out In Decline side
Discard the associated Special Power badge (unless Spirit)
Keep 1 flipped In Decline token per Region, remove the rest
Only 1 In Decline race at a time; remove previous In Decline tokens first

bullets: No conquests on the turn you go In Decline

bullets: Game ends when Game Turn marker reaches last spot and all players finish

Most coins wins; tie-break: most Race tokens (Active + In Decline) on the board

walkthrough.title: Welcome and Setup
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round of Small World with three players. Three players is the recommended count for learning. We will call them Player 1, Player 2, and Player 3. The board should be set up with the 3-player map. The six Race and Special Power combos are visible in a column. Each player starts with five Victory coins. Follow along with the moves even if your group has a different number of players.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 Picks a Combo
walkthrough.text: Player 1 goes first. They look at the six combos in the column. The top combo is free, but it is Fortified Ratmen, which does not excite them. The third combo down is Mounted Humans, and Humans earn bonus coins for Farmland Regions while Mounted lets them conquer Hills and Farmlands for 1 less token. To pick the third combo, Player 1 drops 1 Victory coin on the first combo and 1 coin on the second combo. That costs 2 of their 5 starting coins, leaving them with 3. Player 1 takes the Mounted Humans banner and badge, then collects Race tokens from the tray. Humans show 5 on the banner and Mounted shows 5 on the badge, for 10 tokens total.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 Conquers Regions
walkthrough.text: Player 1 must enter through a border Region. They spot a Farmland border Region with a Lost Tribe on it. That costs 2 base plus 1 for the Lost Tribe equals 3 tokens. Player 1 places 3 Humans there and removes the Lost Tribe to the tray. Next, Player 1 conquers an adjacent empty Hill. Because of the Mounted power, Hills cost 1 less, so this empty Hill costs only 1 token instead of 2. Player 1 places 1 Human token. Then Player 1 takes an adjacent Farmland with 1 Lost Tribe. Normally 3 tokens, but Mounted makes Farmlands cost 1 less, so it costs only 2 tokens. Player 1 places 2 tokens, removes the Lost Tribe. Player 1 has used 6 tokens so far, with 4 remaining. They conquer one more empty Farmland adjacent for 1 token with the Mounted discount. Total: 4 Regions conquered, 7 tokens deployed, 3 remaining in hand for redeployment.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 Redeploys and Scores
walkthrough.text: Player 1 has 3 tokens left in hand. They redeploy these to reinforce their Regions. They stack 2 extra on the Farmland with the Lost Tribe border as protection, and 1 extra on the Hill. Now Player 1 scores. They have 4 Regions, so that is 4 base Victory coins. Plus the Humans earn 1 bonus coin per Farmland Region. Player 1 occupies 3 Farmlands, so that is 3 bonus coins. Total: 7 Victory coins this turn. Add those to the 3 coins they had left after paying for the combo. Player 1 now has 10 coins.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 Picks a Combo
walkthrough.text: Player 2 is up. The column has shifted. The top combo now has 1 Victory coin sitting on it from Player 1's payment. Player 2 decides to grab the top combo. It is free, and they pocket the 1 coin that was sitting on it. Player 2 now has 6 coins. The combo is Fortified Ratmen. Ratmen have no special power but come with a huge number of tokens. The Race banner shows 8 and Fortified shows 3, giving Player 2 a total of 11 Ratmen tokens. Plus the Fortified power lets them place 1 Fortress per turn for extra defense and a bonus coin.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 Conquers Regions
walkthrough.text: Player 2 enters through a border Region on the opposite side of the map from Player 1. They find an empty Swamp border Region, costing 2 tokens. Then they conquer an adjacent Forest with a Lost Tribe for 3 tokens. Then another adjacent empty Region for 2 tokens. Then a fourth Region with a Mountain for 3 tokens. With 11 tokens, Player 2 has conquered 4 Regions using 10 tokens with 1 token remaining. They place a Fortress in the Mountain Region using their Fortified power.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 Scores
walkthrough.text: Player 2 redeploys their 1 remaining token to shore up their weakest Region. They score 4 Regions for 4 base coins, plus 1 bonus coin from the Fortress for the Fortified power. Total: 5 Victory coins. Added to their 6 coins, Player 2 has 11 coins.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Mistake: Spending Too Much on a Combo
walkthrough.text: Player 3 looks at the column and falls in love with the fifth combo down. It is Dragon Master Elves. To get it, Player 3 drops 1 coin on each of the four combos above it, spending 4 of their 5 starting coins. They pocket any coins that might be sitting on the fifth combo from earlier. The problem is Player 3 now has almost no coins left. Those 4 coins they dropped on the combos above will be pocketed by other players later. Meanwhile, Player 1 got a strong combo for only 2 coins, and Player 2 got a free combo plus a bonus coin.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Don't Overpay for Combos
walkthrough.text: Here is the lesson. The combos further down the column can be tempting, but the cost adds up fast. Every coin you spend on a combo is a coin you give to a future opponent when they pick one of the combos above it. In most cases, the top three combos offer the best value. If you really want a combo in the fourth or fifth slot, make sure its Race and Special Power are so well-synergized that the extra points will more than cover what you spent. Dragon Master Elves is powerful, but 4 coins is a steep price in a game where every coin counts toward your final score.

walkthrough.title: Player 3 Conquers and Scores
walkthrough.text: Player 3 makes the best of it. They enter the board and conquer 3 Regions, using their Dragon Master power to take one heavily defended Region with a single token plus the Dragon. The Dragon makes that Region immune to conquest. Player 3 scores 3 base coins plus any Race or Special Power bonuses. Elves do not provide a direct bonus but protect their tokens from being permanently lost. Player 3 ends with a modest score this round.

walkthrough.title: Turn 2 Begins: Ready Your Troops
walkthrough.text: The first player moves the Game Turn marker up one spot. Now each player Readies their Troops. Leave 1 token in each occupied Region and take the rest back in hand. Player 1 picks up their reinforcements from their Regions, keeping 1 Human in each of their 4 Regions and taking 6 tokens back in hand. These 6 tokens are ready for new conquests.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Mistake: Forgetting to Ready Troops
walkthrough.text: Player 3 makes their second mistake. They forget to Ready their Troops before conquering. They leave all their tokens in their existing Regions and try to conquer new Regions with zero tokens in hand. You cannot conquer anything with zero tokens. Always remember: Ready your Troops first by pulling back all but 1 token per Region.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Ready Your Troops
walkthrough.text: Every turn except the one you go In Decline, your first move is to Ready your Troops. Leave 1 token in each Region, take everything else back. The more Regions you hold, the fewer tokens you have available for new conquests, because you are leaving 1 behind in each. This is the core tension of Small World: expanding gives you more coins, but stretches your army thinner.

walkthrough.title: Player 3's Mistake: Holding On Too Long
walkthrough.text: By Turn 4, Player 3's Elves are spread thin across 5 Regions. They only have 1 token available to conquer after Readying Troops, which is not enough to take any defended Region. Player 3 stubbornly holds on instead of going In Decline, hoping to scrape by with 5 coins per turn. Meanwhile, Player 1 went In Decline on Turn 3 and picked a fresh race with a full set of tokens. Player 1 is now scoring from both their new race and their In Decline Humans.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Know When to Decline
walkthrough.text: The most important skill in Small World is knowing when to put your race In Decline. If you cannot conquer new Regions effectively, you are just treading water. The longer you wait, the more turns you waste earning the same stagnant income. Going In Decline at the right time lets you pick a fresh combo with a full set of tokens, conquer aggressively again, and score from both your new race and your old In Decline tokens. Most experienced players decline their first race by Turn 3 or 4.

walkthrough.title: Continue Playing
walkthrough.text: Play continues turn by turn. On each turn, the first player advances the Game Turn marker. Each player either expands through new conquests or goes In Decline, then scores. The cycle of picking races, conquering, declining, and picking again is the heartbeat of the game. Watch your opponents and target their In Decline tokens when possible, since those are only defended by a single token per Region.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have now seen all the core mechanics: picking a combo, conquering Regions, scoring, readying troops, going In Decline, and picking a new race. You watched Player 3 learn three key lessons. Don't overpay for combos. Always Ready your Troops. Know when to Decline. You are ready to play a full game on your own.

walkthrough.title: Before You Conquer
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics on when to decline, which combos to prioritize, and how to read the board.

Second, reference the Appendix for a complete guide to all 14 Races, all 20 Special Powers, and key game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun conquering!

bullets: Scripted tutorial with 3 players on the 3-player map (recommended for learning)
Covers a full first round plus key Turn 2-4 moments
Follow along even if your group has a different player count

Those 4 coins placed on higher combos will benefit future opponents

Every coin dropped on higher combos becomes an opponent's gain

summary.title: Player 3 Conquers and Scores

bullets: First action every turn: leave 1 token per Region, take the rest back
More Regions = fewer available tokens (core tension of the game)
Only skip Readying when going In Decline

summary.title: Player 3's Mistake: Holding On Too Long

bullets: If you can't conquer new Regions, you're wasting turns
Declining gives you a fresh set of tokens and a new combo
Score from both your new race AND In Decline tokens
Most experienced players decline their first race by Turn 3 or 4

summary.title: Continue Playing

Target opponents' In Decline tokens — only 1 token defending each Region

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You've seen all core mechanics in action

summary.title: Before You Conquer
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics on when to decline and which combos to prioritize
Reference the Appendix for all 14 Races, 20 Special Powers, and key terms
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime — tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we get into strategy, here are the most common mistakes new Small World players make. First, holding onto a dying race for too many turns. If you cannot conquer at least one new Region per turn, your race is costing you coins. Second, overpaying for combos in the fourth or fifth slot. Every coin you drop on the combos above becomes a gift to an opponent. Third, forgetting to Ready your Troops. Always pull back all but one token per Region before conquering. Fourth, ignoring In Decline tokens on the board. They are easy targets with only one token per Region. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the biggest beginner traps.

walkthrough.title: When to Go In Decline
walkthrough.text: This is the single most important decision in Small World. A good rule of thumb: if you have fewer tokens available after Readying Troops than the number of tokens needed to conquer one defended Region, it is time to decline. Another signal is when other players are eyeing your territory and you do not have enough tokens to defend it. Going In Decline is not giving up. It is a strategic reset. You keep scoring from your In Decline tokens while starting fresh with a powerful new race. Most experienced players decline their first race around Turn 3 or 4, and may decline a second time around Turn 6 or 7 in longer games.

walkthrough.title: Evaluating Race and Special Power Combos
walkthrough.text: When choosing a combo, consider three things. First, token count. The sum of the Race banner value and Special Power badge value determines how many troops you get. Higher is generally better because more tokens means more Regions conquered. Second, look at synergy. Does the Special Power complement the Race power? Mounted Humans is strong because the conquest discount helps you reach more Farmlands for bonus coins. Third, consider the map situation. If the board is full of Mountains, Giant power becomes very strong. If coastlines are open, Seafaring or Tritons shine. Always look at the board before picking a combo.

walkthrough.title: Territory and Positioning
walkthrough.text: Where you conquer matters as much as how many Regions you take. Regions in the center of the map are surrounded by many neighbors and hard to defend. Border and corner Regions have fewer exposed edges. Mountains are always worth targeting because they provide a permanent defensive bonus, requiring one extra token for anyone to conquer. Try to create a contiguous block of Regions that is hard to attack from multiple directions rather than a long thin chain with exposed flanks.

walkthrough.title: Maximizing Coin Income
walkthrough.text: The base rule is simple: 1 coin per Region. But the bonuses from Race and Special Power abilities can double or even triple your income. Humans plus Farmlands, Wizards plus Magic Regions, Dwarves plus Mines, the Hill Special Power plus Hill Regions. When choosing a combo, count how many matching terrain types are available on the board. If there are five Forests on the map and you have the Forest Special Power, that is 5 bonus coins every single turn. These terrain bonuses are the most reliable way to pull ahead.

walkthrough.title: Reading Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to what other players are doing. If someone just went In Decline, their new race will enter from a border Region, so try not to be sitting on the edge of the map with a thin defense. If someone has the Sorcerer power, do not leave lone tokens in Regions adjacent to their Sorcerers, or they will convert your token for free. If an opponent has the Diplomat power, they might protect themselves from your attacks. Understanding opponents' Race and Special Power abilities is critical to defending your own territory.

walkthrough.title: Targeting In Decline Tokens
walkthrough.text: In Decline tokens are the most efficient targets on the board. Each In Decline Region has only a single token defending it, so it costs just 2 Race tokens to conquer, plus 1 for the defending token, equaling 3 total. Compare that to Active Regions where an opponent may have stacked 3 or 4 tokens. By conquering In Decline Regions, you gain territory cheaply while denying your opponent passive income. Always scan the board for In Decline targets before attacking Active races.

walkthrough.title: Using the Reinforcement Die Wisely
walkthrough.text: The Reinforcement Die is a powerful tool for your final conquest each turn. Its faces show 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, and 3, so you have a 50 percent chance of rolling at least a 1. Always plan your conquests so your last one can take advantage of the die. Conquer the easy Regions first with exact token counts, then use your remaining token plus the die for a stretch target. Even with just 1 token left, you can potentially take an empty Region if you roll a 1 or higher. Never waste the die opportunity by being too conservative with your earlier conquests.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't hold a dying race too long — if you can't conquer, decline
Don't overpay for combos in the 4th or 5th slot
Always Ready your Troops: leave 1 per Region, take the rest back
Target In Decline tokens — they're the easiest conquests on the board

summary.title: When to Go In Decline
bullets: If Ready Troops gives you fewer tokens than 1 conquest costs, decline
Decline is a strategic reset, not giving up
You keep scoring from In Decline tokens while picking a new race
Most players decline their first race by Turn 3 or 4

summary.title: Evaluating Race and Special Power Combos
bullets: Token count = Race banner value + Special Power badge value (higher is better)
Look for synergy: Race + Special Power that complement each other
Consider the board: match terrain bonuses to available Regions
Top 3 combos are usually the best value

bullets: Border and corner Regions have fewer exposed edges — easier to defend
Mountains provide permanent +1 defense to their occupant
Build contiguous blocks rather than long thin chains
Central Regions are high-traffic and hard to hold

Terrain bonuses: Humans+Farmland, Wizards+Magic, Dwarves+Mine, etc.
Count available matching terrain types on the board before choosing a combo
Consistent terrain bonuses are the most reliable path to high scores

summary.title: Reading Your Opponents
bullets: Track opponents' Race and Special Power abilities
Don't leave lone tokens near Sorcerers — they'll convert them
When an opponent declines, expect their new race to enter from a border
Diplomat power can shut down your attacks — adjust targets accordingly

summary.title: Targeting In Decline Tokens
bullets: In Decline Regions have only 1 defending token
Cost to conquer: 2 base + 1 for token = 3 Race tokens
Much cheaper than attacking Active Regions with 3-4 stacked tokens
Denies opponents passive income every turn

summary.title: Using the Reinforcement Die Wisely
bullets: Die faces: 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3 — 50% chance of at least 1
Conquer easy targets first with exact tokens, save die for a stretch target
Even 1 token + die can take an empty Region on a roll of 1+
Never skip the die opportunity — it's a free bonus conquest attempt

walkthrough.title: The Decline Tempo
walkthrough.text: The most important strategic concept in Small World is decline tempo. Every race has a natural lifespan: you pick it, conquer aggressively for 2-3 turns, then your tokens are spread thin and you are earning diminishing returns. The optimal decline timing depends on how many tokens you picked up versus how many you have left, and whether your bonus income from Race and Special Powers is still meaningful. Going In Decline too early wastes a race with life left in it. Going In Decline too late wastes turns earning below-average income. The sweet spot is usually when your Ready Troops phase gives you fewer than 3 available tokens.

walkthrough.title: Coin Denial: Attacking In Decline Tokens
walkthrough.text: One of the strongest moves in Small World is targeting opponents' In Decline Regions. Each In Decline Region earns its owner 1 coin per turn passively. By conquering those single-token Regions for just 3 tokens each, you simultaneously gain income and destroy an opponent's passive revenue stream. A player who loses all their In Decline tokens loses what could be 3-5 free coins every turn. Prioritize wiping out In Decline tokens of the leading player. This is especially powerful with races like Sorcerers, who can replace a lone token for free.

walkthrough.title: Combo Evaluation: Token Count Is King
walkthrough.text: When evaluating the six visible combos, the total token count is the single most important factor. More tokens means more Regions conquered on your first turn, which means higher income from the start. A combo with 12 tokens and a weak power will often outperform a combo with 7 tokens and a strong power. The exception is combos with extremely strong terrain bonuses that multiply every turn, like Merchant or Hill paired with a race that naturally seeks those terrain types. As a rule of thumb, any combo with 10 or more tokens is strong, and combos below 7 tokens need an exceptional power to justify.

walkthrough.title: Power Synergies: Best Combos
walkthrough.text: Some Race and Special Power combos are significantly stronger than others. Mounted Humans get a conquest discount on Farmlands, which also earn bonus coins for Humans. Wizard plus Hill gives bonus coins from both Magic and Hill Regions. Commando Skeletons conquer with fewer tokens and gain bonus Skeleton tokens for each pair of Regions captured. Sorcerers with any high-token-count Special Power can convert lone enemy tokens and grow their army. Always look for combos where the Race and Special Power both push you toward the same Regions or strategy.

walkthrough.title: Map Control and Chokepoints
walkthrough.text: Each map has natural chokepoints where a few heavily defended Regions can control access to a large section of the board. Mountains are natural fortresses. Regions surrounded by Seas or Lakes have fewer attack vectors. When conquering, think about which Regions are hardest for opponents to reach. Stacking 3-4 tokens in a Mountain Region behind a natural barrier can protect an entire section of your territory for multiple turns. The Trolls excel at this strategy because their Troll's Lair adds permanent defense that survives even when going In Decline.

walkthrough.title: The Stout Power Advantage
walkthrough.text: The Stout Special Power is one of the most underrated abilities in the game. Normally, going In Decline costs you an entire turn with no conquests. Stout lets you conquer and score normally, then go In Decline at the end of that same turn. This means you save an entire turn of income that would otherwise be wasted on the decline action. Over the course of the game, one extra productive turn can be worth 8-12 coins. If you see Stout in the column, seriously consider picking it regardless of the paired race.

walkthrough.title: The Spirit Power and Double Decline
walkthrough.text: The Spirit Special Power breaks one of the game's fundamental rules. Normally, you can only have one race In Decline at a time, and putting a new race In Decline removes the previous one. Spirit tokens never count toward this limit. If your Spirit race goes In Decline, those tokens stay on the board permanently, even when you decline a future race. This means you can have two sets of In Decline tokens earning passive income. Maximize Spirit by conquering many Regions before declining, since every Region keeps earning for the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: The Ghoul Strategy
walkthrough.text: Ghouls are unique because all their tokens remain on the board when going In Decline instead of the usual 1 per Region. Even more remarkably, In Decline Ghouls can continue conquering new Regions in following turns, playing as if they were still Active. This happens at the start of your turn, before your new Active race conquers. With Ghouls, you effectively have two conquering armies simultaneously. The optimal play is to conquer aggressively with Ghouls, go In Decline with a large board presence, then use your In Decline Ghouls as a secondary army while your new race handles the primary offense.

walkthrough.title: Multiplayer Dynamics and Kingmaking
walkthrough.text: In games with 3-5 players, diplomacy matters. Attacking the leader is generally correct, but be careful not to weaken yourself in the process while a third player profits. The ideal attack targets In Decline tokens of the leader, which costs minimal tokens. Avoid engaging in prolonged wars with an Active race, as the mutual token losses benefit everyone else. When two opponents are fighting each other, expand into uncontested territory. And remember: final scores are hidden, so the player who seems to be winning might not actually be leading.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Timing
walkthrough.text: The last 2-3 turns of the game require a shift in thinking. Going In Decline becomes less valuable because you have fewer turns to earn back the investment. If your current race still has 2-3 available tokens after Readying Troops and 2 turns remain, it is usually better to keep conquering rather than declining. Similarly, Fortresses, Troll Lairs, and other defensive pieces become more valuable in the endgame because you only need them to survive 1-2 more rounds. Stack your defenses, fortify your best Regions, and maximize your per-turn income for the home stretch.

summary.title: The Decline Tempo
bullets: Every race has a 2-3 turn lifespan before diminishing returns
Decline when Ready Troops gives you fewer than 3 available tokens
Too early = wasted race potential; too late = wasted turns at low income
Most players should decline 2-3 times in a full game

summary.title: Coin Denial: Attacking In Decline Tokens
bullets: In Decline Regions cost only 3 tokens to conquer (2 base + 1 defender)
Destroying passive income hurts opponents every remaining turn
Prioritize wiping out the leading player's In Decline tokens
Sorcerers can convert lone In Decline tokens for free

summary.title: Combo Evaluation: Token Count Is King
bullets: Total tokens = Race banner value + Special Power badge value
10+ tokens = strong combo; below 7 needs exceptional power
More tokens = more Regions = higher first-turn income
Exception: terrain-bonus combos can outperform high-token combos over time

summary.title: Power Synergies: Best Combos
bullets: Mounted Humans: discount + bonus on the same terrain (Farmlands)
Commando Skeletons: fewer tokens to conquer + bonus Skeleton tokens
Sorcerers + high-token power: convert enemies and grow army
Look for Race + Power pushing toward the same Regions

summary.title: Map Control and Chokepoints
bullets: Mountains and Seas create natural defensive barriers
Stack 3-4 tokens in Mountain Regions behind chokepoints
Trolls excel at area control: Troll's Lair adds permanent +1 defense
Fewer exposed borders = fewer tokens needed for defense

summary.title: The Stout Power Advantage
bullets: Stout lets you conquer, score, AND go In Decline on the same turn
Saves a full turn of income vs. normal decline (worth 8-12 coins)
One of the most underrated Special Powers
Consider picking Stout regardless of paired race

summary.title: The Spirit Power and Double Decline
bullets: Spirit In Decline tokens never get removed by future declines
Can have 2 sets of In Decline tokens earning passive income
Maximize: conquer many Regions before declining
Every Spirit In Decline Region earns coins for the rest of the game

In Decline Ghouls can still conquer new Regions each turn

Conquer aggressively, decline with full board, then use as secondary army

Avoid prolonged wars with Active races — mutual losses help third parties

Keep conquering if you have 2-3 available tokens and 2 turns remain
Defensive pieces (Fortresses, Troll Lairs) increase in value near game end
Stack defenses, fortify best Regions, maximize per-turn income

entries.definition: Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). Four Amazon tokens are for conquest only, not defense. At end of each Troop Redeployment, remove 4 tokens from the map. Take them back in hand when you Ready your Troops next turn. Effectively gives you 4 extra tokens for attacking each turn.

Race. 3 base tokens (banner value 3). Each Mine Region your Dwarves occupy earns 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn. This bonus is kept even when Dwarves are In Decline, making them one of the few races with lasting In Decline value.

Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). When an enemy conquers one of your Regions, keep all your Elf tokens in hand for redeployment instead of discarding 1 token. Elves never lose tokens to enemy conquest, making them very resilient.

Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). All Ghoul tokens stay on the map when going In Decline instead of the usual 1 per Region. In Decline Ghouls can continue conquering new Regions as if still Active. Conquests must happen at start of turn, before Active race.

Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). May conquer any Region adjacent to a Mountain Region they occupy at a cost of 1 less Giant token. A minimum of 1 Giant is still required. Strong when Mountains are centrally located.

Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). May enter the map through any Region, not just border Regions. Place a Hole-in-the-Ground in each of the first 2 Regions conquered, making them immune to enemy conquests and racial/special powers. Holes-in-the-Ground are removed when Halflings go In Decline or abandon those Regions.
Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). Each Farmland Region your Humans occupy earns 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn. Strong on maps with many Farmlands.

Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). Each non-empty Region your Orcs conquered this turn earns 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn. Rewards aggressive conquest of occupied Regions rather than empty ones.
Race. 8 base tokens (banner value 8). No special race benefit. Their strength is sheer numbers — 8 base tokens is the highest of any race, allowing them to conquer many Regions on the first turn.

Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). During Troop Redeployment, take 1 new Skeleton token from the storage tray for every 2 non-empty Regions conquered this turn. If no tokens remain in the tray, no bonus. Army grows each turn through conquest.

Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). Once per turn per opponent, you may conquer a Region by substituting one of your opponent's Active tokens with one of your own from the storage tray. The target must be a lone Race token in its Region, and the Region must be adjacent to your Sorcerers. Replaced token goes back to the tray.

Race. 6 base tokens (banner value 6). May conquer all Coastal Regions (bordering a Sea or Lake) at a cost of 1 less Triton token. A minimum of 1 Triton is still required. Strong on maps with long coastlines.

Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). Place a Troll's Lair in each Region your Trolls occupy. The Troll's Lair adds +1 defense (like an extra token). Troll's Lair remains even when Trolls go In Decline. Removed if you abandon the Region or an enemy conquers it.
Race. 5 base tokens (banner value 5). Each Magic Region your Wizards occupy earns 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn.
Special Power. +4 tokens. Collect 2 bonus Victory coins at the end of each turn your race has not yet gone In Decline. A consistent bonus that rewards staying Active as long as possible.

Special Power. +4 tokens. You may use the Reinforcement Die before each of your conquests, not just the last one. Roll the die first, select the Region, then place required tokens minus the die result. If not enough tokens remain, this is your final conquest. Minimum 1 token still required.

Special Power. +5 tokens. Deploy 5 Encampment tokens in any of your Regions during Troop Redeployment. Each Encampment counts as +1 defense. Multiple Encampments can be placed in one Region. Encampments are never lost during attacks — redeployed at end of current player's turn. Disappear when race goes In Decline.
Special Power. +4 tokens. You may conquer any Region with 1 less Race token than normal. A minimum of 1 token is still required. Effectively reduces every conquest cost by 1.

Special Power. +5 tokens. At the end of your turn, select one opponent whose Active race you did not attack this turn. You are at peace with them and they cannot attack your Active race until your next turn. Tokens In Decline are not protected. Ghouls In Decline are immune and can still attack you.

Special Power. +5 tokens. Once per turn, conquer a Region using a single Race token regardless of the number of defenders. Place the Dragon in the conquered Region, making it immune to enemy conquests and powers. Dragon moves to a new Region each turn. Dragon disappears when you go In Decline.

Special Power. +5 tokens. You may conquer any Region of the map except Seas and Lakes. Regions do not need to be adjacent or contiguous to ones you already occupy. Removes the adjacency requirement for conquests.
Special Power. +4 tokens. Collect 1 bonus Victory coin for each Forest Region you occupy at end of turn.

Special Power. +3 tokens. Once per turn, while your Fortified race is Active, place 1 Fortress in a Region you occupy. The Fortress is worth 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn (unless In Decline). Fortress adds +1 defense even when In Decline. Max 1 Fortress per Region, max 6 Fortresses on the map total. Removed if you abandon the Region or an enemy conquers it.

Special Power. +5 tokens. At end of your turn, place each of your 2 Heroes in 2 different Regions you occupy. Those Regions are immune to enemy conquests and powers until your Heroes move. Heroes disappear when you go In Decline.
Special Power. +4 tokens. Collect 1 bonus Victory coin for each Hill Region you occupy at end of turn.

Special Power. +2 tokens. Collect 1 bonus Victory coin for any Region you occupy at end of turn. Effectively doubles your income from every Region. Low token count is offset by massive coin generation.
Special Power. +5 tokens. You may conquer any Hill or Farmland Region with 1 less Race token than normal. A minimum of 1 token is still required.
Special Power. +5 tokens. Each non-empty Region you conquer this turn is worth 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn. Rewards aggressive attacks on occupied Regions.

Special Power. +5 tokens. While your Seafaring race is Active, you may conquer Seas and the Lake, treating them as 3 empty Regions (cost 2 tokens each). You keep these Regions even In Decline and continue scoring for them. Only Seafaring races may occupy Seas and the Lake.

Special Power. +5 tokens. When your Spirit race goes In Decline, those tokens never count toward the one-In-Decline-race limit. You may have two different races In Decline on the map simultaneously. If a third race declines, Spirit tokens remain while the other In Decline race is removed as normal. Spirit In Decline tokens only leave through enemy conquests.
Special Power. +4 tokens. You may go In Decline at the end of a regular turn of conquests, after scoring, instead of spending an entire turn going In Decline. Saves a full turn of lost income.
Special Power. +4 tokens. Collect 1 bonus Victory coin for each Swamp Region you occupy at end of turn.

Special Power. +5 tokens. You may conquer any Region with a Cavern with 1 less Race token than normal. A minimum of 1 token is still required. All Cavern Regions are also considered adjacent to each other for your conquest purposes, creating a teleportation network.
Special Power. +4 tokens. Collect 7 bonus Victory coins once only, at the end of your first turn. A massive one-time bonus that front-loads your income.

The rectangular piece representing a race. Colored side up when Active, grayed-out side up when In Decline. Shows the base number of Race tokens for that race. 14 Race banners in the base game plus 1 blank.

The round-edged piece that pairs with a Race banner. Shows the additional number of Race tokens granted by the power. 20 Special Power badges in the base game plus 1 blank. Discarded when a race goes In Decline (except Spirit).
The round pieces representing your troops on the map. 168 total across all 14 races. Colored side up when Active, grayed-out side up when In Decline. Used to conquer and hold Regions.

18 tokens placed on the map during setup on Regions marked with the Lost Tribe symbol. They represent ancient civilizations In Decline. Each Lost Tribe adds 1 to a Region's conquest cost. Discarded when conquered.
9 tokens placed during setup on Regions with Mountain symbols. Mountains are immovable and remain permanently. They add +1 to conquest cost and provide defense for whoever holds the Region.

Placed by Trolls in each Region they occupy. Adds +1 defense (like an extra token). Stays even when Trolls go In Decline. Removed when an enemy conquers the Region or if you abandon it. 10 available in the game.

Placed by the Fortified Special Power. Adds +1 defense and earns 1 bonus Victory coin at end of turn (while Active only). Maximum 1 per Region, 6 total on the map. Removed when conquered or abandoned.

Used by the Bivouacking Special Power. 5 tokens total. Each adds +1 defense. Multiple can be placed in one Region. Never lost during attacks — redeployed at end of current player's turn. Disappear when race goes In Decline.

Used by Halflings. 2 tokens total. Placed in the first 2 Regions Halflings conquer. Makes those Regions immune to enemy conquests and racial/special powers. Removed when Halflings go In Decline or abandon the Region.
Used by the Dragon Master Special Power. 1 token. Placed in a conquered Region to make it immune to enemy conquests and powers. Moves to a new Region each turn. Removed when you go In Decline.

Used by the Heroic Special Power. 2 tokens. Placed in 2 different Regions you occupy at end of turn. Those Regions are immune to enemy conquests and powers until Heroes move. Removed when you go In Decline.

The custom die used for final conquest attempts. Has faces showing 0, 0, 0, 1, 2, and 3. Rolled once per turn for your last conquest attempt if you are 1-3 tokens short. Berserk power lets you roll before every conquest.

109 coins in four denominations: 30 tens, 24 fives, 20 threes, and 35 ones. Kept hidden from other players. Used to pay for combos and accumulated as score. Players may request change from the stash at any time.
Tracks game progress on the Game Turn track. Advances one spot at the start of each turn by the first player. Game ends when it reaches the last spot and all players finish that turn.
Printed on the board. Shows the total number of turns in the game: 8, 9, or 10 depending on the player count. The Game Turn marker moves along this track.

A bounded area on the map that can be conquered and occupied. Each Region has a terrain type and may contain Mountains, Lost Tribes, or other features. Seas and Lakes are not conquerable Regions unless you have the Seafaring power.
A Region adjacent to the edge of the board, or one whose shore is on a Sea adjacent to the edge. Your first race must enter the map by conquering a Border Region.
A Region bordering a Sea or Lake. Tritons conquer Coastal Regions for 1 less token. Relevant for the Seafaring power as well.
Your currently active race on the map with colored-side-up tokens. You conquer with Active Race tokens and receive Race and Special Power bonuses. You can only have one Active race at a time.

A race that has been retired. Its tokens are flipped to the grayed-out side, 1 per Region. In Decline tokens score 1 coin per Region but generally no Race or Special Power bonuses. Only 1 In Decline race per player at a time (exception: Spirit).
The first action on each turn after the first. Leave 1 Race token in each occupied Region and take all others back in hand. These returned tokens are available for conquests this turn.
After conquests end, freely redistribute your Race tokens across all Regions you occupy. No adjacency requirement. Must leave at least 1 token in each Region you wish to keep.
When a race enters the map for the first time, it must conquer a Border Region. This rule only applies to the initial entry — subsequent conquests follow the adjacency rule instead.

A Region containing at least one Lost Tribe or Race token (Active or In Decline). A Region with only a Mountain marker and no tokens is considered empty. Relevant for Orcs, Pillaging, Skeletons, and Sorcerers.
A terrain type on the map. Humans earn 1 bonus coin per Farmland they occupy. Mounted power gives a conquest discount on Farmlands.
A terrain type on the map. The Hill Special Power earns 1 bonus coin per Hill Region occupied. Mounted power gives a conquest discount on Hills.
A terrain type on the map. The Forest Special Power earns 1 bonus coin per Forest Region occupied.
A terrain type on the map. The Swamp Special Power earns 1 bonus coin per Swamp Region occupied.
A terrain type on the map. Always has a Mountain token placed on it during setup. Adds +1 to conquest cost. Giants get a conquest discount on Regions adjacent to Mountains they occupy.
A terrain feature on the map. Dwarves earn 1 bonus coin per Mine Region they occupy, even when In Decline.
A terrain feature on the map (Magic Region). Wizards earn 1 bonus coin per Magic Region they occupy.
A terrain feature on the map. The Underworld Special Power gives a conquest discount on Cavern Regions and treats all Caverns as adjacent to each other.
A water area on the map. Cannot normally be conquered. Seafaring races may conquer Seas, treating them as empty Regions costing 2 tokens each.
A water area on the map. Cannot normally be conquered. Seafaring races may conquer the Lake, treating it as an empty Region. Regions bordering the Lake are Coastal for Triton purposes.
